BOTTLENECK: latency-bound
REASONING: The kernel is essentially a no-op stub that performs no GPU computation—it only calls `cudaDeviceSynchronize()` and a host-side `puts`. The 0.0% occupancy and 0.0% DRAM utilization confirm that no actual GPU work is being launched, so the entire execution time is dominated by host-side latency (API call overhead and synchronization). There is no compute or memory work to bottleneck on.
